title: Text flickers a lot when pulling images.
body:
### Issue Description

Same as #25426 but I can't re-open.

The text (loading bars) flickers a lot and spawns a lot of new lines.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run podman-compose pull



### Describe the results you received

The loading bars flicker a lot 

### Describe the results you expected

Just normal ======= loading bars updating in the same line they first appear. (like pacman)

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I am using arch (btw) with foot terminal and a wayland compositor (Hyprland). But the same happens on ghostty, which is a very modern terminal emulator.

comments.body:
This is the terminal capture to visualize what I'm trying to describe.

[![asciicast](https://asciinema.org/a/EscNjdu87MZOxtOqGj1XKQPwx.svg)](https://asciinema.org/a/EscNjdu87MZOxtOqGj1XKQPwx)

I guess the issue is that podman-compose run several pull command sin parallel on the same terminal? In that case the other commands have no idea of the other commands also flushing the terminal output so they all overwrite each other all the time causing the flickering.

I am not sure that is something podman could fix as the command cannot know if there are other commands also writing to the same terminal. cc @mtrmac
I limited `image_parallel_copied` in containers.conf to 1 and it helped a bit. Now only one line keeps getting replaced. But still flickers a lot.

> I guess the issue is that podman-compose run several pull command sin parallel on the same terminal? In that case the other commands have no idea of the other commands also flushing the terminal output so they all overwrite each other all the time causing the flickering.

Yes.

In principle:
- One _hypothetical_ approach would be to make the concurrent writers aware of each other: run them all in a single process; _then_ we could perhaps write some implementation that coordinates writes to stdout. I don’t know what the API for that would look like … I’d prefer not to expose the library we use for progress bars as a stable API commitment, and we’d need to have a single persistent “progress bar container” while now we, typically, have two per copied platform.
- Another, much more viable, approach would be for `podman-compose` to _not_ direct output directly to the terminal. Send it to a log file or /dev/null, that will deactivate drawing the progress bars entirely. Podman-compose would then need to take on the responsibility for reporting progress; and that, in turn, _sort of_ depends on the progress API exposed by Podman. Some work is happening in that area.

Either way, `podman-compose` will need to change something; this cannot be fixed within Podman alone.

> Another, much more viable, approach would be for podman-compose to not direct output directly to the terminal. Send it to a log file or /dev/null, that will deactivate drawing the progress bars entirely. Podman-compose would then need to take on the responsibility for reporting progress; and that, in turn, sort of depends on the progress API exposed by Podman. Some work is happening in that area.

Well I guess that is the temporary solution to remove the crazy flickering. I can just &> to a file and then tail -f the contents. Thanks for the idea. :)

Run: `podman-compose pull &> logs.txt`
Then in another terminal window: `tail -f logs.txt`
FWIW `podman-compose pull |& cat` might be sufficient if you don’t want the log file.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am closing this since it appears to be a podman-compose issue. Please report in https://github.com/containers/podman-compose

> * Another, much more viable, approach would be for `podman-compose` to _not_ direct output directly to the terminal. Send it to a log file or /dev/null, that will deactivate drawing the progress bars entirely. Podman-compose would then need to take on the responsibility for reporting progress; and that, in turn, _sort of_ depends on the progress API exposed by Podman. Some work is happening in that area.

FWIW that has _just_ landed: #28224 .
